mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate expanding aggregate supply and robust economic activity, which drive corporate earnings growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between INDPRO change and forward 20-day returns is below 0.03, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of months with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, the labor market becomes extremely tight, pushing up wage costs and compressing profit margins, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE < 4.5%, the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high and rising federal funds rate and elevated consumer price inflation signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: When both triggers are met, the forward 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts and retail sales indicates a recovery in residential and consumer demand, which boosts construction employment and consumer spending, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises by more than 5%
failure_condition: After a housing starts surge, the next 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in less than 55% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real retail sales signals strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon without any threshold condition.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth with forward 20-day returns is below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A large monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 indicates robust labor demand and economic momentum, which reassures equity markets and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After a payrolls surge, the forward 20-day return is positive in less than 55% of cases, or the average return is not significantly greater than zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines while industrial production rises, the economy is in a virtuous cycle of rising output and falling slack, which boosts corporate profits and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the forward 60-day return is positive in less than 60% of cases, or the median return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4%, the monetary policy stance is contractionary, which tightens financial conditions and reduces equity risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, the forward 5-day return is positive in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
